purpose: Full lifecycle state machine for any dispatch event, regardless of which mechanism originates it (governance-chain dispatch_executor.py OR roadmap-autopilot.yml direct curl). Closes the false-success risk: an HTTP 204 is not completion.

states.meaning: Task/object selected for dispatch by a router (curator_router.py or roadmap_autopilot.py); no network call made yet
HTTP POST to GitHub Actions workflow_dispatch endpoint made; HTTP 204 received. This is acknowledgement of receipt by GitHub, NOT confirmation the workflow started or finished.
states.writer: dispatch_executor.py OR roadmap-autopilot.yml inline dispatch step

states.meaning: HTTP call did not return 204, or threw a network error
GitHub Actions run_id observed via API (workflow run list query matching trace_id/inputs) confirming the dispatched run actually began executing
states.writer: dispatch_executor.py polling step OR roadmap-autopilot.yml follow-up step

states.meaning: GitHub Actions run concluded with status=success, observed via API
GitHub Actions run concluded with status=failure, observed via API
No START_CONFIRMED or COMPLETED observed within configured TTL after DISPATCHED
The owning SSOT file (ACTIVE_QUEUE.json row, or architecture_plan_fact_gap.json row) has been updated to reflect this outcome via a valid SSOT transaction (ssot_transaction_protocol.json)
End of lifecycle for this dispatch event. No further state transitions permitted.
Selected but never dispatched (operator cancel, circuit breaker, policy block)

hard_rule: release_status=RELEASE_CLEARED is FORBIDDEN for any area whose evidence depends on a dispatch event that has not reached COMPLETED followed by STATE_COMMITTED. DISPATCHED alone (HTTP 204) must never be read as success by any auditor, runner, or report generator.
current_known_gap: Neither dispatch_executor.py nor roadmap-autopilot.yml currently implement START_CONFIRMED polling. Both currently stop logging at DISPATCHED. This is the single most important functional gap exposed by this protocol — see BEM-010 roadmap task DSM-1.
